assumption: Putting a real SUS score improvement in the headline converts better than a descriptive headline for the hiring manager audience.
evidence_for: JTBD-001 says outcomes need numbers. PER-001 frustration: 'improved usability with no number attached.' COMP-001: Simon Pan puts metrics in homepage cards.
evidence_against: SUS is a niche metric. A recruiter at Google may not know if 'high 80s' is good. The story (tab vs modular) may be a stronger signal than the number.
test: Ship both versions. Track which version gets more case study clicks if analytics are added. Or ask hiring managers directly during interviews.
